page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz with a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 300 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieves average ratings in Passmark CPU and memory scores, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks fall within the average tier. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to poor for medium 7B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5dn.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring both local NVMe storage and optimized network throughput. The inclusion of dedicated physical cores and AVX-512 instruction support provides steady execution for vector calculations, though the older DDR4 memory generation and Intel Xeon architecture present a performance tradeoff compared to newer hardware generations.

This instance is configured for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and moderate caching layers that benefit from local NVMe instance storage and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.1 GHz clock speed, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The hardware configuration includes a 300 GB local NVMe SSD instance store and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for multi-core CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution and larger LLM inference tasks show weaker results. Qualitatively, it offers balanced resource density for workloads requiring local storage and steady network throughput. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations.
